info.long_name: Percentage of kindergartners with medical exemptions from MMR vaccination
info.short_description: Percentage of kindergarten children with medical exemptions from MMR vaccination requirements
info.long_description: The percentage of kindergarten students with medical exemptions from state MMR (measles, mumps, rubella) vaccination requirements. Medical exemptions are granted when a physician determines that vaccination poses a health risk to a specific child. This dataset aggregates state-reported exemption data at county and state levels. IMPORTANT: Values are rounded for privacy protection, so small changes may not be reflected in the data. Data coverage and reporting methods vary by state and over time. The study compiled exemption data from all 50 U.S. states and Washington, DC, spanning prepandemic (2009-2019) and postpandemic (2020-2024) periods. NOTE: Alaska, Delaware, District of Columbia, Indiana, and Rhode Island do not have county-level data in the source; for these states, the state-level value has been assigned uniformly to all counties.
info.statement: In {location}, {value}% of kindergartners had medical exemptions from MMR vaccination

citations.title: Fattah M, Stoffel LA, Bubar KM, Bents SJ, Maldonado Y, Hotez PJ, Kiang MV, Lo NC. Trends in County-Level Childhood Vaccination Exemptions in the US. JAMA. 2026 Feb 10;335(6):546-549. doi: 10.1001/jama.2025.24407. PMID: 41533386; PMCID: PMC12805488.

info.long_name: Percentage of kindergartners with non-medical exemptions from MMR vaccination
info.short_description: Percentage of kindergarten children with non-medical exemptions from MMR vaccination requirements
info.long_description: The percentage of kindergarten students with non-medical (religious or philosophical) exemptions from state MMR (measles, mumps, rubella) vaccination requirements. Non-medical exemptions are based on personal, religious, or philosophical beliefs rather than medical contraindications. Availability of non-medical exemptions varies by state. IMPORTANT: Values are rounded for privacy protection, so small changes may not be reflected in the data. Data coverage and reporting methods vary by state and over time. The study compiled exemption data from all 50 U.S. states and Washington, DC, spanning prepandemic (2009-2019) and postpandemic (2020-2024) periods. NOTE: Alaska, Delaware, District of Columbia, Indiana, and Rhode Island do not have county-level data in the source; for these states, the state-level value has been assigned uniformly to all counties.
info.statement: In {location}, {value}% of kindergartners had non-medical exemptions from MMR vaccination

citations.title: Fattah M, Stoffel LA, Bubar KM, Bents SJ, Maldonado Y, Hotez PJ, Kiang MV, Lo NC. Trends in County-Level Childhood Vaccination Exemptions in the US. JAMA. 2026 Feb 10;335(6):546-549. doi: 10.1001/jama.2025.24407. PMID: 41533386; PMCID: PMC12805488.

year.short_description: Year in which vaccination exemption was recorded
year.long_description: The calendar year for which kindergarten vaccination exemption data was reported by state health departments. Data spans from 2009 through 2024, with coverage varying by state and year.
year.restrictions: Attribution required - Cite Kiang et al. 2025

sources.name: Fattah M, Stoffel LA, Bubar KM, Bents SJ, Maldonado Y, Hotez PJ, Kiang MV, Lo NC. Trends in County-Level Childhood Vaccination Exemptions in the US. JAMA. 2026 Feb 10;335(6):546-549. doi: 10.1001/jama.2025.24407. PMID: 41533386; PMCID: PMC12805488.

time_period.long_name: Classification of time relative to COVID-19 pandemic
time_period.short_description: Whether data is from prepandemic or postpandemic period
time_period.long_description: A categorical variable classifying the year as either 'prepandemic' (2009-2019) or 'postpandemic' (2020-2024). This classification allows for analysis of how medical exemption rates changed before and after the COVID-19 pandemic, which may have affected vaccination attitudes, healthcare access, and exemption practices.
time_period.restrictions: Attribution required - Cite Kiang et al. 2025

sources.name: Fattah M, Stoffel LA, Bubar KM, Bents SJ, Maldonado Y, Hotez PJ, Kiang MV, Lo NC. Trends in County-Level Childhood Vaccination Exemptions in the US. JAMA. 2026 Feb 10;335(6):546-549. doi: 10.1001/jama.2025.24407. PMID: 41533386; PMCID: PMC12805488.

county.long_name: Name of county reporting exemption data
county.long_description: The name of the county for which vaccination exemption data was reported. This field is only present in the county-level data file. County-level data availability varies by state, as not all states report exemption data at the county level.
county.restrictions: Attribution required - Cite Kiang et al. 2025

citations.title: Fattah M, Stoffel LA, Bubar KM, Bents SJ, Maldonado Y, Hotez PJ, Kiang MV, Lo NC. Trends in County-Level Childhood Vaccination Exemptions in the US. JAMA. 2026 Feb 10;335(6):546-549. doi: 10.1001/jama.2025.24407. PMID: 41533386; PMCID: PMC12805488.

fattah_exemptions.name: Medical Exemptions From Childhood Vaccination in the US (Kiang et al. 2025)
fattah_exemptions.description: A comprehensive study of medical vaccine exemption rates among U.S. kindergartners from 2009-2024, published in JAMA by Kiang et al. The study compiled exemption data from all 50 U.S. states and Washington, DC, providing state- and county-level medical exemption rates for MMR vaccination. The dataset spans prepandemic (2009-2019) and postpandemic (2020-2024) periods, enabling analysis of how exemption patterns changed over time and following the COVID-19 pandemic. Medical exemptions are granted when a physician determines that vaccination poses a health risk to a specific child, distinct from religious or philosophical exemptions. Values are rounded for privacy protection. The research was conducted in collaboration with NBC News and methodology is documented in an accompanying article. Full data and code are available on GitHub.
fattah_exemptions.restrictions: Attribution required. Cite Fattah M, Stoffel LA, Bubar KM, Bents SJ, Maldonado Y, Hotez PJ, Kiang MV, Lo NC. Trends in County-Level Childhood Vaccination Exemptions in the US. JAMA. 2026 Feb 10;335(6):546-549. doi: 10.1001/jama.2025.24407. PMID: 41533386; PMCID: PMC12805488.
